australia.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
summary: Summarize this news article:
Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.
In next week's Farrer by-election, the Coalition parties are preferencing One Nation over the leading local independent. Many are wondering why.
The official line from Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan is that Hanson's party is the "least worst" option.
Independent Michelle Milthorpe, they argue, is really a "teal" (a label Milthorpe rejects), who would be bad news for farmers in Farrer.
But there's an unspoken reason too, which goes to the longer game, and the prospects of winning the seat back.
The Coalition has argued independent Michelle Milthorpe is really a "teal", who would be bad news for farmers in Farrer.(ABC Goulburn-Murray: Annie Brown)
First, it's worth noting how both the Liberal and National party leaders are managing expectations ahead of this by-election test.
Neither is talking up the prospects of a Coalit Summarize this news article:
ion win. Far from it. They have both spent time on the ground and can read the polls.
Taylor is not falling into the trap former leader Peter Dutton set for himself three years ago when he said the Aston by-election would be a "verdict on the leaders, no doubt about that".
The Liberals went on to lose the by-election. Dutton at least fronted up the next morning on Insiders to accept responsibility.
By contrast, Taylor is laying the groundwork for a likely defeat.
Whenever asked about his party's chances in Farrer, he talks about the "big mountain to climb", and the fact it "takes time" to rebuild trust.
In other words, no one should hold their breath for a surprise Liberal comeback victory in the May 9 by-election.
Both the Liberal and National party leaders are managing expectations ahead of this by-election test.(ABC News: Keane Bourke)
Matt Canavan, who has only been leader of the Nationals for seven weeks, is similarly realistic. The Queenslander, who has at least managed to grab s

australia.title: See how the price of everything has changed
summary: Summarize this news article:
The cost of most everyday items is up — here's by how much.
If it feels like (almost) everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is.
The impact of the Iran war is seeping through the economy, with Treasurer Jim Chalmers saying Australians are already paying a "hefty price" for the war.

This week, headline inflationjumped to 4.6 per cent. That's the highest it's been since September 2023, when the nation's economy was rebounding after the COVID-19 pandemic. And it raises the chances of more interest rate rises.
Below, we've tracked exactly what's going on with prices —from beef and books to furniture and insurance.

australia.title: 'Accident' helps identify giant 'shark-toothed' dinosaur's skull fragment
summary: Summarize this news article:
Two fossils found in Thailand have been identified as long-missing pieces of a "shark-toothed" dinosaur first discovered less than 20 years ago.
TheSiamraptor suwati— first unveiled by scientists in 2019 — was the first predatory dinosaur of its kind found in South-East Asia.
A new analysis of a controversial tyrannosaur fossil reveals the dinosaur wasn't a teenage version of T. rex as once thought, but instead a distinct miniature dinosaur.
A new peer-reviewed report published in scientific journal PLOS One this week has identified the braincase and a partial skull fragment of the carnivore.
The pieces were recovered from a rock formation in north-eastern Thailand by the Japan-Thailand Dinosaur Project, "a large therapod braincase" sometime before 2007 and "a partial skull roof" in 2007.
The discovery of their origin, lead researcher Soki Hattori told the ABC, was partly accidental.
"I became involved in this research after 2018 … at that point, the braincase specimen had an unknown p Summarize this news article:
rovenance," he said.
"The skull roof was known to come from the same locality asSiamraptor… this, in turn, gave us confidence that the braincase was also fromSiamraptor."
Separate research confirmed the braincase was part ofSiamraptor— from 2023, research moved "rapidly".
"The braincase allows us to research the shape of the brain, which appears to be typical for this group of dinosaurs," said Dr Hattori, an associate professor at Fukui Prefectural Museum.
TheSiamraptorhas been estimated to be around 8 meters in length with "shark-like" teeth, one of the carcharodontosaurids.
Some of the largest land predators ever known, other carcharodontosaurids includedGiganotosaurusandTyrannotitan, rivalling theTyrannosaurusandSpinosaurusin size.
TheSiamraptorhas previously been described as a basal member of this group, meaning it evolutionally split from other carcharodontosaurids very early on.
For scientists, it represents a missing link.
That is according to Dr Mike Lee, a professor of evolut

india.title: Exit polls decoded: Bengal cliffhanger, Cong return in Kerala & status quo in TN, Assam & Puducherry
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: West Bengal could be on the cusp of "poriborton" with the exit polls predicting a hung assembly that could mark the end of Mamata Banerjeeâs 15-year-old rule in the state. Most of the exit polls projected a neck-and-neck race between the ruling Trinamool
And the other big surprise, if one pollster is to be believed, could come from the south where actor-politician
âs TVK may end the DMK-AIADMK duopoly in Tamil Nadu by emerging as the single largest party. However, most of the other pollsters have given a clear edge to the DMK-Congress alliance in the state.
Exit Polls Show Razor-Close Bengal Battle, Thin Swing Could Decide BJP vs TMC Outcome
In Assam, the BJP is predicted to score a hat-trick of victories with a big win over the Congress, which continues to struggle in the northeastern state.

While in Kerala, the CPM-led LDF may be on its way out marking the end of the only Left government in the country. In Puducherry, the ruling NDA is likely to retain power with a com Summarize this news article:
prehensive victory over the Congress-DMK alliance.

Exit polls released on Wednesday after the conclusion of voting across four states and one Union Territory (UT) offered an indecisive picture, where entrenched incumbencies, rising challengers and new entrants may simultaneously shape electoral outcomes if the exit polls numbers hold.
With results scheduled for May 4, the projections reflect both continuity and churn across regions, underscoring how state-specific dynamics continue to define voter behaviour.
In West Bengal, the numbers point to one of the most fiercely fought contests in recent memory.

While individual exit polls vary, the broader trend suggests a sharply polarised electorate split almost evenly between the TMC and the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), which left no stone unturn to dent Mamata's citadel.
A poll of polls placed both parties at approximately 145 seats each in the 294-member assembly, with smaller parties and independents expected to secure only a marginal pr

india.title: Iran 'choking like a stuffed pig': Trump digs in on Hormuz blockade
summary: Summarize this news article:

has said the United States will maintain its naval blockade on Iran until Tehran agrees to a deal addressing Washingtonâs concerns over its nuclear programme, signalling a hardening stance even as military options remain under consideration.

Speaking to Axios, Trump indicated that the blockade is currently his preferred tool of pressure, describing it as more effective than direct military action. âThe blockade is somewhat more effective than the bombing. They are choking like a stuffed pig. And it is going to be worse for them. They can't have a nuclear weapon,â he said.

The US position comes amid Iranâs proposal to first reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, while deferring nuclear negotiations to a later stage, an approach Trump has rejected. He said Tehran is seeking relief from the blockade but maintained that it would not be lifted without firm guarantees on its nuclear programme.
Meanwhile, United States Central Command has already drawn up plans for a âsh Summarize this news article:

ort and powerfulâ wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure, according to Axios sources familiar with the matter. The strikes are intended to break the current negotiating deadlock and push Iran back to the table under greater pressure.
However, as of Tuesday night, Trump had not authorised any kinetic action.

For now, the administration sees the blockade as its primary leverage, though officials indicated that military action remains an option if negotiations fail to progress. Trump declined to discuss operational details but suggested that the pressure campaign is already taking a toll, claiming Iranâs oil infrastructure is under strain due to its inability to export.

Iran, however, has pushed back strongly against the continued US blockade in Hormuz. A senior Iranian security source told state media that the US naval blockade would âsoon be met with practical and unprecedented action,â warning that restraint shown so far may not continue if the pressure persists.

india.title: IPL: SRH chase 244! Head, Klaasen overshadow Rickelton’s historic MI ton in run-fest
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: In a jaw-dropping run-fest at the Wankhede Stadium,
powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to a sensational six-wicket win over
, chasing down a massive 244 with eight balls to spare in IPL 2026.

The duoâs explosive effort overshadowed a record-breaking unbeaten 123 from Ryan Rickelton, whose knock had earlier lifted Mumbai to 243/5 â their highest total batting first and one that seemed imposing on paper.
Chasing 244, SRH made a mockery of the target, racing to 249/4 in just 18.4 overs in what became the fourth-highest successful chase in IPL history.

Head set the tone with a blistering 76 off just 30 balls, smashing eight sixes and four boundaries. However, his innings was aided by multiple lifelines, with MI dropping chances and missing a crucial edge early on.
Naman Dhir was involved in two missed opportunities, while Rickelton failed to spot an edge behind the stumps â moments that proved costly as Head punished the hosts.
After Headâs dismissal, Klaasen took over with a b Summarize this news article:
rutal unbeaten 65 off 30 balls, dismantling the bowling attack with seven fours and four sixes.
He found support in Salil Arora, who finished with a rapid 30 not out off 10 balls to seal the chase in emphatic fashion.
Even Jasprit Bumrah (0 for 54) endured a rare off day, conceding heavily as SRH batters attacked relentlessly from the outset.

Earlier, Rickelton produced one of the finest knocks in Mumbai Indiansâ history, remaining unbeaten on 123 off 55 balls, laced with 10 fours and eight sixes. It was not only MIâs highest individual score but also their fastest century in IPL history.
He was well supported by Will Jacks, who hammered 46 off 22 balls, and skipper Hardik Pandya, who chipped in with a quickfire 31 off 15 deliveries.

The opening stand of 93 in just seven overs laid the foundation, with MI reaching 78 in the powerplay. Rickelton anchored the innings throughout, ensuring the hosts posted a daunting total on a batting-friendly surface.
Despite Rickeltonâs heroics, M

india.title: TN exit polls: AK breakthrough or PK's 'farsh' moment — what will it be for Vijay?
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: Exit poll projections for Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) have thrown up one of the most intriguing questions of the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections: is
on the cusp of a breakthrough like Arvind Kejriwal in Delhi, or will he resemble Prashant Kishor's Bihar experiment that ended in 'arsh se farsh par'.
The answer, at least today, depends on which exit poll one chooses to believe.
Exit Polls Show Razor-Close Bengal Battle, Thin Swing Could Decide BJP vs TMC Outcome
Most projections place TVK in the 10â24 seat range, a significant debut. According to most pollsters Vijay's party will be one that positions it as a spoiler rather than a main contender.
P-Marq and Matrize projected 10â12 seats for TVK, while Peoples Pulse gives it a stronger 18â24, suggesting meaningful traction among urban and youth voters.
In this scenario, Vijayâs party could end up splitting anti-incumbent votes, indirectly aiding the DMK.
Axis MyIndia projected a dramatic surge, giving TVK 98â120 seats -  Summarize this news article:
numbers that, if turned into reality on May 4, would catapult Vijay into the centre of Tamil Nadu politics overnight.
That would place him in Kejriwal territory: a first-time entrant not just breaking through, but becoming the centre of state politics.
And Vijay also has his superstar Thalaivar image - something that has worked in Tamil Nadu for years with the rise of MG Ramachandran and the J Jayalalithaa.

However, unlike Delhi in 2013, where Kejriwal rode a singular anti-corruption wave, Tamil Nadu's contest is layered, entrenched in Dravidian politics, strong party machinery, and decaded-old voter loyalties. Even a strong showing for TVK does not automatically translate into power unless it converts popularity into booth-level efficiency, an area where established players like the DMK and AIADMK retain a clear edge.
However, if these exit polls turn out to be wrong on May 4, and Vijay's grand political debut becomes a flop show, it would be similar to what we saw last year in Bihar.
